_patch_meta.description: vcb-010 re-evaluation — Session B observation flags. Three flags raised: (1) SB_FINDING for apistia (G0570) on 8-group inner-being-role fragmentation across 11 verses; (2) SB_FINDING for spoudē (G4710) on 11-group fragmentation across 12 verses; (3) PH2_BOUNDARY_QUESTION for apeitheō (G0544) on the disbelief/disobedience straddle and the registry-165/registry-50 boundary. Companion VCSDPOINTERS patch carries the cross-registry pointer for G0544 (165 → 50).

record.flag_label: apistia (G0570) operates across 8 distinct inner-being roles in 11 NT verses
record.description: Re-evaluation under wa-versecontext-instruction-v3_8 strict-grouping discipline found that apistia (G0570, mti=1198) fragments into 8 characteristic-distinct groups across 11 active verses (highest verse-to-group ratio in vcb-010). The eight groups capture distinct inner-being roles: (1198-001) corporate covenantal unbelief with covenantal-historical limitation/exclusion [Mat 13:58, Mar 6:6, Rom 11:20, Heb 3:19 anchor]; (1198-002) individual self-acknowledged unbelief co-existing with faith [Mar 9:24 anchor sole]; (1198-003) disciples' post-resurrection unbelief under apostolic-formation rebuke [Mar 16:14 anchor sole]; (1198-004) hypothetical covenantal infidelity as rhetorical foil to God's faithfulness [Rom 3:3 anchor sole]; (1198-005) the inner state in negated role — what did not arise to govern Abraham [Rom 4:20 anchor sole]; (1198-006) continuing-but-not-permanent corporate unbelief opening to restoration [Rom 11:23 anchor sole]; (1198-007) autobiographical pre-conversion unbelief as ground for received mercy [1Ti 1:13 anchor sole]; (1198-008) heart's susceptibility to an unbelieving condition leading to apostasy [Heb 3:12 anchor sole]. Session B implication: the synthesis of 'unbelief' for this term cannot reduce to a single characterisation — the analyst must integrate eight materially distinct roles. Pattern note: G4710 spoudē (mti=1268) shows the same kind of high spread (11 groups for 12 verses) in registry 181 zeal — see the parallel SB_FINDING flag for that term.

record.flag_label: spoudē (G4710) operates across 11 distinct inner-being roles in 12 NT verses
record.description: Re-evaluation under wa-versecontext-instruction-v3_8 strict-grouping discipline found that spoudē (G4710, mti=1268) fragments into 11 characteristic-distinct groups across 12 active verses (highest verse-to-group ratio in vcb-010 alongside apistia). STEP/Mounce already distinguishes two lexical layers: physical haste, and inner earnestness/zeal/diligence; the corpus shows further differentiation. The eleven groups: (1268-001) physical haste expressing negative-moral inner urgency [Mar 6:25 anchor sole]; (1268-002) physical haste expressing positive-obedient inner eagerness [Luk 1:39 anchor sole]; (1268-003) author's inner-eagerness as urgency-of-writing-desire [Jude 3 anchor sole]; (1268-004) inner zeal as right Christian disposition for body-life — fervency vs sloth [Rom 12:8, 12:11 anchor]; (1268-005) earnestness as inner-being fruit produced by godly grief [2Cor 7:11 anchor sole]; (1268-006) earnestness revealed under apostolic disciplinary writing [2Cor 7:12 anchor sole]; (1268-007) earnestness as one virtue in catalogue of excellence [2Cor 8:7 anchor sole]; (1268-008) earnestness as evidence of genuine love [2Cor 8:8 anchor sole]; (1268-009) earnest care as God-given inner disposition placed in the heart [2Cor 8:16 anchor sole]; (1268-010) earnestness oriented toward eschatological assurance [Heb 6:11 anchor sole]; (1268-011) inner effort directed at moral-formative supplementation [2Pe 1:5 anchor sole]. Session B implication: synthesis of 'zeal' for this term must integrate physical-haste, body-life-zeal, repentance-fruit, virtue-catalogue, evidence-of-love, God-given-care, eschatological-readiness, and supplementation-effort as separable inner-being roles. Pattern note: G0570 apistia shows the same kind of high spread (8 groups for 11 verses) in registry 165 unbelief — see the parallel SB_FINDING flag.

record.flag_label: apeitheō (G0544) straddles disbelief and disobedience — registry 165 ↔ 50 boundary
record.description: Re-evaluation surfaced a registry-boundary question for apeitheō (G0544, mti=1199). STEP/Mounce defines the verb as 'to be uncompliant; to refuse belief, disbelieve (Jn 3:36); to refuse belief and obedience (Rom 10:21; 1Pe 3:20); to refuse conformity (Rom 2:8)'. The Greek term does not separate 'disbelief' and 'disobedience' the way English does — the same verb expresses both inner orientations across the corpus. Within registry 165 (unbelief) the term has been fitted to 5 characteristic-distinct groups: (1199-001) refusal of the Son/word/gospel — inner-being refusal directed at Christ-revelation [Joh 3:36 anchor, 1Pe 2:8, 3:1, 4:17]; (1199-002) active unbelief expressing as hostility against gospel-bearers [Act 19:9 anchor, Act 14:2, Rom 15:31]; (1199-003) refusal of moral truth — inner non-conformity that prefers unrighteousness [Rom 2:8 anchor sole]; (1199-004) corporate covenantal disobedience in salvation-history [Rom 10:21 anchor, Heb 3:18, Heb 11:31, 1Pe 3:20]; (1199-005) covenantal disobedience as relational state in Paul's mercy-logic [Rom 11:30 anchor, Rom 11:31]. The XREF table on G0544 lists G0545 apeithēs (disobedient) as OWNER in registry 50 (disobedient). Session B implication: the registry-165 analytical home for G0544 needs to be reconciled with the registry-50 home of the cognate adjective G0545. The split between groups 1199-001/-002 (refusal-of-Christ-revelation/active-hostility) and 1199-003/-004/-005 (refusal-of-moral-truth/covenantal-disobedience/mercy-logic) suggests the verb's weight in registry 165 may need explicit boundary articulation — what is in scope as 'unbelief' versus what shades toward 'disobedience' for downstream synthesis. See companion VCSDPOINTERS SD_POINTER flag for the cross-registry connection (165 → 50).
